reasoning: A is the strongest: it names the actual decision (attribute substitution tell), runs an explicit outside-view base rate with a competing reference class, isolates the true hinge (does the work generalize), does a concrete premortem, generates option C, and uniquely attaches a calibrated 65% confidence plus the three numbers that would change the answer. B covers nearly the same ground (steelmanned case-for, dev-shop path dependency, milestone counter) with solid burn math but states no probability and surfaces fewer cognitive traps explicitly. C is competent and gives the cleanest decline-versus-advance-product comparison and a tight term sheet, but it asserts more than it interrogates: thinner on counter-arguments, no premortem or base-rate framing, and the least bias-awareness work of the three.
